branches.condition: Developer chooses to skip Domain Authentication and use Single Sender Verification for testing.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Allows sending test emails without full domain authentication but not recommended for production.
branches.path: Testing path with Single Sender Verification

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer sends a valid email message through the SendGrid API and receives a successful response.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: HTTP 202 Accepted response status code from the API indicating the email was accepted for delivery.
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully send the first email using the SendGrid Mail Send API.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official documentation explicitly defines sending the first email via the API as the initial success milestone and provides sample code and curl commands to achieve this.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has access to an API key or can create one
Developer has basic knowledge of HTTP requests or programming language SDK usage
entry_point.developer_intent: Send an email programmatically using the SendGrid API.

friction_gates.description: Must create a SendGrid account to access API and dashboard.
Two-factor authentication (2FA) is required to secure the account.
API key with Mail Send permissions must be created and stored securely.
Domain Authentication is required to verify sender identity for production use.
Developer must have programming environment and optionally install SendGrid SDK.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a SendGrid account to access the API and dashboard.
Enable Two-factor authentication (2FA) on the SendGrid account.
Create and store a SendGrid API Key with Mail Send > Full Access permissions.
Complete Domain Authentication to verify sender identity for production use.
Install required programming language SDK or have tools to make HTTP requests (e.g., curl).

primary_path.action: Sign up for a SendGrid account.
details: Visit the SendGrid website and create a new account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Registration errors or email verification pending.

primary_path.action: Enable Two-factor authentication (2FA) on the account.
details: Choose 2FA method: SMS or Authy app.
primary_path.input: Phone number or Authy app setup.

primary_path.action: Create an API key with Mail Send > Full Access permissions.
details: Navigate to API key creation page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Permission errors or key creation failure.

primary_path.action: Complete Domain Authentication to verify sender identity.
details: Add DNS records as instructed by SendGrid.
Wait for DNS propagation and verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: DNS propagation delay or misconfiguration errors.

primary_path.action: Set environment variable SENDGRID_API_KEY with the API key value.
details: Export the API key in the shell environment or configure in application settings.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Environment variable not set or inaccessible.

primary_path.action: Write code to send an email using the SendGrid Mail Send API or SDK.
details: Use sample code from official docs for preferred language.
primary_path.input: Email message parameters and API key.

primary_path.action: Run the code or curl command to send the email.
details: Execute the program or curl command in terminal or runtime environment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network errors, API key issues, or spam filtering delays.
primary_path.input: Compiled or interpreted code or curl command.
primary_path.success_signal: Email received in recipient inbox or HTTP 202 response.

evidence_supported: API endpoint and usage for sending email
Requirement of API key in Authorization header
sources.title: SendGrid v3 API reference

evidence_supported: Documentation structure and focus on API usage
sources.title: Twilio SendGrid developer documentation

evidence_supported: Code examples for sending first email
sources.title: Twilio SendGrid

evidence_supported: Step-by-step instructions and code for sending email
sources.title: Twilio SendGrid | SendGrid Docs | Twilio
Getting started with the SendGrid API

alternatives_considered.name: SendGrid UI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The UI is for manual email campaign management and not the primary developer onboarding surface for API integration.

alternatives_considered.name: SendGrid SDKs
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SDKs are wrappers around the Web API; the API itself is the fundamental integration surface.

surface.name: SendGrid v3 Web API
surface.selection_basis: The official SendGrid documentation and API reference emphasize the v3 Web API as the primary interface for developers to send email programmatically.

uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor-claimed or documented time estimate, it is unclear how quickly a new developer can expect to send their first email.
uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending first email) as claimed by SendGrid?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation does not provide explicit timing or duration estimates for onboarding or first email send.
